title: asplenium ceterach subsp. ceterach
intro: Asplenium ceterach subsp. ceterach, also known as the wall-rue or false spleenwort, is a type of fern native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Asplenium ceterach subsp. ceterach is also valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
